name: CARDINAL HEALTH INC
summary: Cardinal Health, Inc. is a global healthcare services and products company founded in 1979 in Ohio. It distributes branded, generic, and specialty pharmaceuticals, and manufactures and distributes medical, surgical, and laboratory products. The company serves hospitals, healthcare systems, pharmacies, ambulatory surgery centers, clinical laboratories, and physician offices. It operates through two main segments: Pharmaceutical and Specialty Solutions and Global Medical Products and Distribution, plus nuclear pharmacies, at-home medical supplies, and logistics services.

products.name: Pharmaceutical Distribution
products.description: Distribution of branded, generic, specialty pharmaceuticals, and OTC healthcare products in the United States.

products.name: Cardinal Health Brand Medical Products
products.description: Manufactured and sourced medical, surgical, and laboratory products sold globally.

products.name: Nuclear and Precision Health Solutions
products.description: Manufactures, prepares, and delivers radiopharmaceuticals for nuclear imaging, theranostics, and other procedures.

products.name: Xofigo
products.description: Contract-manufactured radiopharmaceutical treatment produced by Cardinal Health.

products.name: Lymphoseek
products.description: Radiopharmaceutical diagnostic imaging agent; Cardinal Health holds North American manufacturing and distribution rights.

products.name: at-Home Solutions (Edgepark/ADS)
products.description: Provides medical supplies directly to patients with chronic conditions in the home, including diabetes therapies.

products.name: OptiFreight Logistics
products.description: Shipping and logistics optimization for healthcare providers through integrated technology solutions.

products.name: Navista
products.description: Managed services organization enhancing efficiency and profitability for independent community oncologists.

products.name: The Specialty Alliance (GIA/Urology America)
products.description: MSO platform serving gastroenterologists and urologists across hundreds of practice locations in multiple states.

partnerships.description: Distribution contracts that expired during fiscal 2025, previously contributing significant Pharma segment revenue.
